title: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent on Cleveland's East Side
metaTitle: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent Cleveland East Side
metaDescription: Find 3 bedroom houses for rent on Cleveland's east side — Slavic Village, Buckeye-Shaker, Fairfax and more. Rent ranges, transit, Section 8-friendly homes.
primaryKeyword: 3 bedroom houses for rent cleveland east side
secondaryKeywords: section 8 3 bedroom house east side cleveland
answerBox: 3-bedroom houses on Cleveland's east side — including Slavic Village, Buckeye-Shaker, Fairfax, and Mount Pleasant — typically rent from about $1,100 to $1,500 a month. The east side is where we work with the largest share of our 90+ rental homes, and every one accepts Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers.

sections.h2: 3-bedroom houses for rent on Cleveland's east side
body: Cleveland's east side covers a wide swath of the city, from Hough and Fairfax near University Circle, north into Glenville and Collinwood, and southeast through Buckeye-Shaker, Mount Pleasant, Lee-Harvard, and Slavic Village. It's the part of Cleveland where we work with the largest concentration of our rental homes, and 3-bedroom houses are our second most common size behind 2-bedrooms.

Our east-side inventory runs heaviest in **ZIP codes 44105 (Slavic Village/Union-Miles), 44110 (Collinwood), 44108 (Glenville), 44104 (Central/Fairfax), 44103 (Hough), and 44120 (Buckeye-Shaker)**. If you need a 3-bedroom specifically, [book a showing](/p/book-showing) and share your target ZIP or neighborhood so we can check what's currently open.

sections.h2: How much does a 3-bedroom house rent for on the east side?
body: Across our current availability, 3-bedroom homes run **$1,100 to $1,500 a month**, averaging around $1,250. That's close to Zumper's citywide median of $1,350 for 3-bedrooms reported July 4, 2026, and well below RentCafe's July 2, 2026 average of $2,716, which reflects larger professionally-managed apartment complexes rather than standalone rental houses.

rows: Median asking rent, all Cleveland listings
Average of professionally-managed apartment complexes, 1,483 sq ft

body: Fairfax sits adjacent to the Cleveland Clinic's main campus, and University Circle — home to Case Western Reserve University, the Cleveland Museum of Art, and Severance Music Center — is a short distance from both Fairfax and Hough. The **HealthLine**, RTA's bus-rapid-transit route along Euclid Avenue, connects Public Square Downtown through University Circle to East Cleveland in a dedicated median lane. Farther southeast, Buckeye-Shaker runs along the **RTA Blue and Green light-rail lines**, which terminate in Shaker Heights.
Slavic Village, on the far southeast side, has its own recreational corridor along the Morgana Run Trail, and is close to I-77 and I-490 for drivers heading south or east.

items: **Rail transit:** RTA Blue/Green Lines through Buckeye-Shaker to Shaker Heights; Red Line further north through University Circle to East Cleveland
**Bus rapid transit:** HealthLine, Euclid Avenue, Public Square to University Circle
**Landmarks:** Cleveland Clinic main campus (Fairfax), University Circle institutions, Shaker Square, Morgana Run Trail (Slavic Village)

body: Every home we work with on the east side accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. CMHA, the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority, accepts [preliminary voucher applications online](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) year-round, with placement made through a random mini-lottery process rather than strict first-come, first-served — so timelines vary from a few days to several months.

It's worth knowing that **Cleveland proper does not have a source-of-income ordinance** requiring landlords to accept vouchers; that protection currently applies only in Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale. Our voucher acceptance across every unit is a company policy, not a citywide mandate. More detail is in our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

sections.h2: What a 3-bedroom east-side rental typically includes
body: Housing stock in Slavic Village, Mount Pleasant, and Buckeye-Shaker tends to be early-1900s single-family houses and duplexes, often with full basements and small yards — a 3-bedroom layout here commonly means a bit more room than a 2-bedroom for the same style of home rather than a different building type. As with our full [Cleveland houses for rent](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) listings, expect a mix of standalone houses and duplex or triplex units rather than an all-detached inventory; compare against our [2-bedroom east side listings](/cleveland-rentals/houses/2-bedroom-houses-for-rent-cleveland-east-side/) if a smaller unit also works for your household.

faq.q: What east-side ZIP codes have the most 3-bedroom houses for rent?
faq.a: Our largest east-side concentrations are ZIP codes 44105 (Slavic Village), 44110 (Collinwood), 44108 (Glenville), 44104 (Central/Fairfax), 44103 (Hough), and 44120 (Buckeye-Shaker). Exact 3-bedroom availability changes regularly, so book a showing to see current options.

faq.q: How much does a 3-bedroom house rent for on Cleveland's east side?
faq.a: Our current east-side 3-bedroom availability runs roughly $1,100 to $1,500 a month, averaging about $1,250. Zumper's July 2026 citywide median for 3-bedroom rentals is $1,350, while RentCafe's average for larger managed apartment complexes runs much higher at $2,716.

faq.q: Do east-side 3-bedroom rentals accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with on the east side accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Cleveland itself has no ordinance requiring voucher acceptance citywide, so this is a standing policy on our part, not a universal local requirement.

faq.q: What transit options serve east-side 3-bedroom rentals?
faq.a: The RTA Blue and Green Lines run through Buckeye-Shaker to Shaker Heights, the Red Line runs through University Circle toward East Cleveland, and the HealthLine bus-rapid-transit route runs along Euclid Avenue connecting Public Square to University Circle.

faq.q: Are east-side 3-bedroom rentals mostly houses or duplexes?
faq.a: It's a mix. Housing stock includes standalone single-family houses alongside duplexes and triplexes, particularly in Slavic Village, Mount Pleasant, and Buckeye-Shaker. Our rental listings reflect that same blend rather than exclusively detached houses.
